content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      :   File                                           Date  :   May 6, 1978


From  :   FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
           Gina S-K

Subject:  Agenda item at opinion request meeting of March 31, 1978


A media business which produces campaign material for sale
to the public for the sole purpose of making money without
an intent to influence the action of voters is not a
committee and has no reporting obligation as a result of
the sale of the material.

State of California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants                Date : 3/21/78

                    Gina S-K
From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject: Item for discussion


     If a media business produces campaign material (buttons,
bumper strips, etc.) for sale to the public for the
sole purpose of making money without an intent to influence
the action of the voters, are the funds received reportable.
The material may or may not expressly advocate the election
or defeat of a candidate or measure.

                                        [illegible]
                                        to log as strictly
                                        money makers of
                                        not for the purpose
                                        of influencing
                                        the action of the
                                        votes.

sections.question: If a media business produces campaign material (buttons, bumper strips, etc.) for sale to the public for the sole purpose of making money without an intent to influence the action of the voters, are the funds received reportable. The material may or may not expressly advocate the election or defeat of a candidate or measure.
sections.conclusion: A media business which produces campaign material for sale to the public for the sole purpose of making money without an intent to influence the action of voters is not a committee and has no reporting obligation as a result of the sale of the material.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a media business that produces and sells campaign materials (such as buttons and bumper strips) to the public solely for profit, without intent to influence voters, must report the funds received and whether such a business constitutes a committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that funds from selling campaign materials are not reportable as long as the media business is strictly profit-motivated and not attempting to influence voter action, and such a business does not qualify as a committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both an internal memo and opinion request meeting materials. The conclusion and question are clearly identified from the source material. Document dated May 6, 1978, concerning agenda item from March 31, 1978 meeting.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If a media business produces campaign material (buttons, bumper strips, etc.) for sale to the public for the sole purpose of making money without an intent to influence the action of the voters, are the funds received reportable. The material may or may not expressly advocate the election or defeat of a candidate or measure.

CONCLUSION: A media business which produces campaign material for sale to the public for the sole purpose of making money without an intent to influence the action of voters is not a committee and has no reporting obligation as a result of the sale of the material.

State of California Memorandum To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants Date : 3/21/78 Gina S-K From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Subject: Item for discussion If a media business produces campaign material (buttons, bumper strips, etc.) for sale to the public for the sole purpose of making money without an intent to influence the action of the voters, are the funds received reportable. The material may or may not expressly advocate the election or defeat of a candidate or measure. [illegible] to log as strictly money makers of not for the purpose of influencing the action of the votes.
embedding.summary: The FPPC addresses whether a media business selling campaign materials (buttons, bumper strips, etc.) to the public for profit has reporting obligations. The FPPC concludes that no reporting is required if the business is solely profit-motivated without intent to influence voters.
